description.value: This value set includes a smattering of Benefit type codes.

copyright.value: This material derives from the HL7 Terminology (THO). THO is copyright ©1989+ Health Level Seven International and is made available under the CC0 designation. For more licensing information see: https://terminology.hl7.org/license

definition.value: The maximum amount payable by the plan for covered services under a specific benefit category or coverage period.
The amount a member must pay for covered services before the plan begins to make payments, subject to the terms of the benefit.
A unit-based benefit representing the number of covered outpatient encounters or services provided by a healthcare professional.
A benefit category representing coverage related to inpatient accommodations, such as room type or level of service.
A fixed amount the member is required to pay for a covered service at the time the service is received.
A percentage-based member cost-sharing amount applied to the cost of a covered service.
The maximum copayment amount that may be applied to a covered service, regardless of calculated cost-sharing amounts.
A benefit category representing coverage for eye examinations and related vision assessment services.
A benefit category representing coverage for eyeglass frames and lenses.
A benefit category representing coverage for contact lenses.
A benefit category representing coverage for primary care services.
A benefit category representing coverage for dispensed medications, including pharmacy services associated with prescription fulfillment.
The amount the insured individual pays, as a set percentage of the cost of covered services, as an out-of-pocket payment to the provider. Example: Insured pays 20% and the insurer pays 80%.

The maximum amount a member is required to pay for covered services during a benefit period. Once this limit is reached, the plan pays 100% of covered costs for the remainder of the period, subject to plan rules.

A benefit category representing a reduction in payable benefits or coverage due to failure to meet plan requirements, such as obtaining required prior authorization or complying with medical management rules.
